{{Short description| part_of of kofun Kofuns in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Mikuriyama Kofun group | native_name = 御厨古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Shorinzan Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shorinzan Kofun | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mikuriyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|43|11|N|137|53|17|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Iwata, Shizuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōkai region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 3rd to 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Mikuriyama Kofun group'''|御厨古墳群|Mikuriyama Kofun gun}} is group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located inbetween the Shingai and Kamata neighborhoods of the city of [[Iwata, Shizuoka]] in the [[Tōkai region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2001.{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/192532|title=御厨古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} ==Overview== The site consists of five large Kofuns, located in the southeastern part of the Iwatahara Plateau on the eastern bank of the [[Tenryū River]], which are part of a large cluster of more than 500 ''kofun'', most of which have now been destroyed by urban encroachment and the construction of the [[Tōkaidō Main Line]] railway tracks, which cuts through the site. The '''Shorinzan Kofun''' (松林山古墳), with a total length of 107 meters, is the largest of the group. It is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located south of the railroad tracks and on the north side of Shimmei Junior High School. It has a 66.5 meter diameter posterior circular portion with a height of ten meters. An excavation was conducted in 1931, at which time a long stone [[burial chamber]] was discovered with a large number of [[grave goods]], including one triangular-rimmed [[bronze mirror]], one bronze mirror depicting four animals, two bronze mirrors depicting flowers, ''[[magatama]]'', tubular beads, weapons (straight swords, iron and bronze spears, daggers) and agricultural tools (axes, sickles, saws),and [[Sue pottery]]] pottery were discovered. The mound was covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and the remains of many ''[[haniwa]]'' were found. From the artifacts, the tumulus is estimate to date from the second half of the 4th century. {{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The '''Takeneyama Kofun''' (高根山古墳) is a {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) with a diameter of 52 meters and height of eight meters. It is also located to the south of the railroad tracks, and is the third largest in the prefecture. The surface was covered with ''fukiishi'' and both cylindrical and pot-shaped ''hanawa''. It was built at the end of the 4th century. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from 1997 to 1998. The '''Mikuridōyama Kofun''' (御厨堂山古墳) is a [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]] with total length of 34.5 meters and height of four meters. A bronze mirror and an iron sword have been recovered from its interior. It is also located to the south of the railroad tracks. It was excavated from 1997 to 1998. The '''Inariyama Kofun''' (稲荷山古墳) and the '''Akihayama Kofun''' (秋葉山古墳) are both located to the north of the [[Tōkaidō Main Line]] railway tracks, behind the Buddhist temple of Renjō-ji, and are survivors of what was once a group of four tumuli. The Inariyama Kofun is a 48-meter long, six meter high keyhole-shaped tumulus and the Akihayama Kofun is a 54-meter diameter, eight meter high circular tumulus. The now-vanished '''Kyōzuka Kofun''' (経塚古墳) was a keyhole-shaped tomb with a length of 90 meters, but was destroyed in the early [[Meiji period]] to make way for the Tōkaidō Main Line railway, during which time an [[Shinju-kyo|ancient bronze mirror]] was recovered. Jar-shaped ''haniwa'' have been recovered from the vicinity. The site is located a five-minute walk from the "Shinmei-mae" bus stop on the Entetsu Bus from [[Iwata Station (Shizuoka)|Iwata Station]] on the [[JR East]] Tōkaidō Main Line. ==Gallery== File:Takaneyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Takaneyama Kofun File:松林山古墳出土 三角縁二神二獣鏡 (模造).JPG|bronze mirror from the Shorinzan Kofun {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+Mikuriyama Kofun group !name !location !Coordinates !type !Size !Artifacts !Date !Notes |- |colspan=8 style="background-color:#C0C0C0;text-align:left"|'''Covered by National Historic Site designation''' |- |'''Shorinzan Kofun'''||rowspan=3|Shingai||{{coords|34|43|10.48|N|137|53|16.70|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark}}||keyhole||length = 107m||bronze mirrors, weapons, beads, ''haniwa''||late 4th century||style="text-align:left"|Excavated 1931 |- |'''Inariyama Kofun'''||{{coords|34|43|22.55|N|137|53|23.23|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark}}||keyhole||length = 48m||pottery||late 4th century||style="text-align:left"|Excavated 1997-1998 |- |'''Akihayama Kofun'''||{{coords|34|43|20.80|N|137|53|23.20|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark}}||Circular||dia = 54m||pottery||late 4th century||style="text-align:left"|Excavated 1997-1998 |- |'''Takaneyama Kofun'''||rowspan=2|Kamata||{{coords|34|43|8.30|N|137|53|20.80|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|}}||Circular||dia = 52m||''haniwa''||4th century||style="text-align:left"|Excavated 1997-1998 |- |'''Mikuridōyama Kofun'''||{{coords|34|43|1.05|N|137|53|27.85|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|}}||keyhole||length = 50m||weapons||5th century||style="text-align:left"|early 20th century |- |colspan=8 style="background-color:#C0C0C0;text-align:left"|'''Others''' |- |'''Kyozuka Kofun'''||rowspan=2|Shingai||{{coords|34|43|18.30|N|137|53|25.30|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark}}||keyhole||length = 90m||bronze mirror||early 4th century||style="text-align:left"|excavated 1985-1886; destroyed by railway construction |- |'''Shingai No.17 Kofun'''
（Renjoji No.5 Kofun)||{{coords|34|43|24.12|N|137|53|20.93|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|}}||circular||diameter = 30m||bronze mirror||early 5th century||style="text-align:left"|now destroyed |- |'''Mekakuyama Kofun'''||Kamata||{{coords|34|42|55.52|N|137|53|26.38|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|}}||Circular||35 x 33m|| ||5th century|| |} ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:Mikuriya Kofun Group}} *[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kunishitei/1002049.html Iwata city home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://kanko-iwata.jp/tanoshimu/tanoshimu-738/ Iwata City tourist information] {{in lang|ja}} [[Category:History of Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:11]][[Category:Mikuriyama Kofun group]][[Category:11]] [[en:Mikuriyama Kofun group]] ] [[sn:Mikuriyama Kofun group]][[sn:Mikuriyama Kofun group]][[sn:Mikuriyama Kofun group]][[sn:Mikuriyama Kofun group]][[sn:Mikuriyama Kofun group]][[sn:Mikuriyama Kofun group]][[sn:Mikuriyama Kofun group]][[sn:Mikuriyama Kofun group]][[sn:Mikuriyama Kofun group]][[sn:Mikuriyama Kofun group]][[sn:Mikuriyama Kofun group]] [[Category:completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Mikuriyama Kofun group]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Mikuriyama Kofun group]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Mikuriyama Kofun group]][[Category:11]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Mikuriyama Kofun group}} [[Category:New]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:New]]

{{Short description| part_of of kofun Kofuns in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Mikuriyama Kofun group | native_name = 御厨古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Shorinzan Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shorinzan Kofun | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mikuriyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|43|11|N|137|53|17|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Iwata, Shizuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōkai region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 3rd to 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Mikuriyama Kofun group'''|御厨古墳群|Mikuriyama Kofun gun}} is group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located inbetween the Shingai and Kamata neighborhoods of the city of [[Iwata, Shizuoka]] in the [[Tōkai region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2001.{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/192532|title=御厨古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} ==Overview== The site consists of five large Kofuns, located in the southeastern part of the Iwatahara Plateau on the eastern bank of the [[Tenryū River]], which are part of a large cluster of more than 500 ''kofun'', most of which have now been destroyed by urban encroachment and the construction of the [[Tōkaidō Main Line]] railway tracks, which cuts through the site. The '''Shorinzan Kofun''' (松林山古墳), with a total length of 107 meters, is the largest of the group. It is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located south of the railroad tracks and on the north side of Shimmei Junior High School. It has a 66.5 meter diameter posterior circular portion with a height of ten meters. An excavation was conducted in 1931, at which time a long stone [[burial chamber]] was discovered with a large number of [[grave goods]], including one triangular-rimmed [[bronze mirror]], one bronze mirror depicting four animals, two bronze mirrors depicting flowers, ''[[magatama]]'', tubular beads, weapons (straight swords, iron and bronze spears, daggers) and agricultural tools (axes, sickles, saws),and [[Sue pottery]]] pottery were discovered. The mound was covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and the remains of many ''[[haniwa]]'' were found. From the artifacts, the tumulus is estimate to date from the second half of the 4th century. {{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The '''Takeneyama Kofun''' (高根山古墳) is a {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) with a diameter of 52 meters and height of eight meters. It is also located to the south of the railroad tracks, and is the third largest in the prefecture. The surface was covered with ''fukiishi'' and both cylindrical and pot-shaped ''hanawa''. It was built at the end of the 4th century. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from 1997 to 1998. The '''Mikuridōyama Kofun''' (御厨堂山古墳) is a [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]] with total length of 34.5 meters and height of four meters. A bronze mirror and an iron sword have been recovered from its interior. It is also located to the south of the railroad tracks. It was excavated from 1997 to 1998. The '''Inariyama Kofun''' (稲荷山古墳) and the '''Akihayama Kofun''' (秋葉山古墳) are both located to the north of the [[Tōkaidō Main Line]] railway tracks, behind the Buddhist temple of Renjō-ji, and are survivors of what was once a group of four tumuli. The Inariyama Kofun is a 48-meter long, six meter high keyhole-shaped tumulus and the Akihayama Kofun is a 54-meter diameter, eight meter high circular tumulus. The now-vanished '''Kyōzuka Kofun''' (経塚古墳) was a keyhole-shaped tomb with a length of 90 meters, but was destroyed in the early [[Meiji period]] to make way for the Tōkaidō Main Line railway, during which time an [[Shinju-kyo|ancient bronze mirror]] was recovered. Jar-shaped ''haniwa'' have been recovered from the vicinity. The site is located a five-minute walk from the "Shinmei-mae" bus stop on the Entetsu Bus from [[Iwata Station (Shizuoka)|Iwata Station]] on the [[JR East]] Tōkaidō Main Line. ==Gallery== File:Takaneyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Takaneyama Kofun File:松林山古墳出土 三角縁二神二獣鏡 (模造).JPG|bronze mirror from the Shorinzan Kofun {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+Mikuriyama Kofun group !name !location !Coordinates !type !Size !Artifacts !Date !Notes |- |colspan=8 style="background-color:#C0C0C0;text-align:left"|'''Covered by National Historic Site designation''' |- |'''Shorinzan Kofun'''||rowspan=3|Shingai||{{coords|34|43|10.48|N|137|53|16.70|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark}}||keyhole||length = 107m||bronze mirrors, weapons, beads, ''haniwa''||late 4th century||style="text-align:left"|Excavated 1931 |- |'''Inariyama Kofun'''||{{coords|34|43|22.55|N|137|53|23.23|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark}}||keyhole||length = 48m||pottery||late 4th century||style="text-align:left"|Excavated 1997-1998 |- |'''Akihayama Kofun'''||{{coords|34|43|20.80|N|137|53|23.20|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark}}||Circular||dia = 54m||pottery||late 4th century||style="text-align:left"|Excavated 1997-1998 |- |'''Takaneyama Kofun'''||rowspan=2|Kamata||{{coords|34|43|8.30|N|137|53|20.80|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|}}||Circular||dia = 52m||''haniwa''||4th century||style="text-align:left"|Excavated 1997-1998 |- |'''Mikuridōyama Kofun'''||{{coords|34|43|1.05|N|137|53|27.85|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|}}||keyhole||length = 50m||weapons||5th century||style="text-align:left"|early 20th century |- |colspan=8 style="background-color:#C0C0C0;text-align:left"|'''Others''' |- |'''Kyozuka Kofun'''||rowspan=2|Shingai||{{coords|34|43|18.30|N|137|53|25.30|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark}}||keyhole||length = 90m||bronze mirror||early 4th century||style="text-align:left"|excavated 1985-1886; destroyed by railway construction |- |'''Shingai No.17 Kofun'''
（Renjoji No.5 Kofun)||{{coords|34|43|24.12|N|137|53|20.93|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|}}||circular||diameter = 30m||bronze mirror||early 5th century||style="text-align:left"|now destroyed |- |'''Mekakuyama Kofun'''||Kamata||{{coords|34|42|55.52|N|137|53|26.38|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|}}||Circular||35 x 33m|| ||5th century|| |} ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:Mikuriya Kofun Group}} *[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kunishitei/1002049.html Iwata city home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://kanko-iwata.jp/tanoshimu/tanoshimu-738/ Iwata City tourist information] {{in lang|ja}} [[Category:History of Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:11]][[Category:Mikuriyama Kofun group]][[Category:11]] [[en:Mikuriyama Kofun group]] ] [[sn:Mikuriyama Kofun group]][[sn:Mikuriyama Kofun group]][[sn:Mikuriyama Kofun group]][[sn:Mikuriyama Kofun group]][[sn:Mikuriyama Kofun group]][[sn:Mikuriyama Kofun group]][[sn:Mikuriyama Kofun group]][[sn:Mikuriyama Kofun group]][[sn:Mikuriyama Kofun group]][[sn:Mikuriyama Kofun group]][[sn:Mikuriyama Kofun group]] [[Category:completion tier 4]][[Category:11]][[sn:Mikuriyama Kofun group]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Mikuriyama Kofun group]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Mikuriyama Kofun group]][[Category:11]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Mikuriyama Kofun group}} [[Category:New]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:New]]

The site is located a five-minute walk from the "Shinmei-mae" bus stop on the Entetsu Bus from [[Iwata Station (Shizuoka)|Iwata Station]] on the [[JR East]] Tōkaidō Main Line. ==Gallery== File:Takaneyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Takaneyama Kofun File:松林山古墳出土 三角縁二神二獣鏡 (模造).JPG|bronze mirror from the Shorinzan Kofun {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+Mikuriyama Kofun group !name !location !Coordinates !type !Size !Artifacts !Date !Notes |- |colspan=8 style="background-color:#C0C0C0;text-align:left"|'''Covered by National Historic Site designation''' |- |'''Shorinzan Kofun'''||rowspan=3|Shingai||{{coords|34|43|10.48|N|137|53|16.70|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark}}||keyhole||length = 107m||bronze mirrors, weapons, beads, ''haniwa''||late 4th century||style="text-align:left"|Excavated 1931 |- |'''Inariyama Kofun'''||{{coords|34|43|22.55|N|137|53|23.23|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark}}||keyhole||length = 48m||pottery||late 4th century||style="text-align:left"|Excavated 1997-1998 |- |'''Akihayama Kofun'''||{{coords|34|43|20.80|N|137|53|23.20|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark}}||Circular||dia = 54m||pottery||late 4th century||style="text-align:left"|Excavated 1997-1998 |- |'''Takaneyama Kofun'''||rowspan=2|Kamata||{{coords|34|43|8.30|N|137|53|20.80|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|}}||Circular||dia = 52m||''haniwa''||4th century||style="text-align:left"|Excavated 1997-1998 |- |'''Mikuridōyama Kofun'''||{{coords|34|43|1.05|N|137|53|27.85|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|}}||keyhole||length = 50m||weapons||5th century||style="text-align:left"|early 20th century |- |colspan=8 style="background-color:#C0C0C0;text-align:left"|'''Others''' |- |'''Kyozuka Kofun'''||rowspan=2|Shingai||{{coords|34|43|18.30|N|137|53|25.30|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark}}||keyhole||length = 90m||bronze mirror||early 4th century||style="text-align:left"|excavated 1985-1886; destroyed by railway construction |- |'''Shingai No.17 Kofun'''
（Renjoji No.5 Kofun)||{{coords|34|43|24.12|N|137|53|20.93|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|}}||circular||diameter = 30m||bronze mirror||early 5th century||style="text-align:left"|now destroyed |- |'''Mekakuyama Kofun'''||Kamata||{{coords|34|42|55.52|N|137|53|26.38|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|}}||Circular||35 x 33m|| ||5th century|| |} ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:Mikuriya Kofun Group}} *[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kunishitei/1002049.html Iwata city home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://kanko-iwata.jp/tanoshimu/tanoshimu-738/ Iwata City tourist information] {{in lang|ja}} [[Category:History of Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:11]][[Category:Mikuriyama Kofun group]][[Category:11]] [[en:Mikuriyama Kofun group]] ] [[sn:Mikuriyama Kofun group]][[sn:Mikuriyama Kofun group]][[sn:Mikuriyama Kofun group]][[sn:Mikuriyama Kofun group]][[sn:Mikuriyama Kofun group]][[sn:Mikuriyama Kofun group]][[sn:Mikuriyama Kofun group]][[sn:Mikuriyama Kofun group]][[sn:Mikuriyama Kofun group]][[sn:Mikuriyama Kofun group]][[sn:Mikuriyama Kofun group]] [[Category:completion tier 4]][[Category:11]][[sn:Mikuriyama Kofun group]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Mikuriyama Kofun group]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Mikuriyama Kofun group]][[Category:11]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Mikuriyama Kofun group}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{Kofun navbar}}

revision.text:
{{Short description| part_of of kofun Kofuns in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Mikuriyama Kofun group | native_name = 御厨古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Shorinzan Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shorinzan Kofun | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mikuriyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|43|11|N|137|53|17|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Iwata, Shizuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōkai region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 3rd to 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Mikuriyama Kofun group'''|御厨古墳群|Mikuriyama Kofun gun}} is group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located inbetween the Shingai and Kamata neighborhoods of the city of [[Iwata, Shizuoka]] in the [[Tōkai region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2001.{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/192532|title=御厨古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} ==Overview== The site consists of five large Kofuns, located in the southeastern part of the Iwatahara Plateau on the eastern bank of the [[Tenryū River]], which are part of a large cluster of more than 500 ''kofun'', most of which have now been destroyed by urban encroachment and the construction of the [[Tōkaidō Main Line]] railway tracks, which cuts through the site. The '''Shorinzan Kofun''' (松林山古墳), with a total length of 107 meters, is the largest of the group. It is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located south of the railroad tracks and on the north side of Shimmei Junior High School. It has a 66.5 meter diameter posterior circular portion with a height of ten meters. An excavation was conducted in 1931, at which time a long stone [[burial chamber]] was discovered with a large number of [[grave goods]], including one triangular-rimmed [[bronze mirror]], one bronze mirror depicting four animals, two bronze mirrors depicting flowers, ''[[magatama]]'', tubular beads, weapons (straight swords, iron and bronze spears, daggers) and agricultural tools (axes, sickles, saws),and [[Sue pottery]]] pottery were discovered. The mound was covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and the remains of many ''[[haniwa]]'' were found. From the artifacts, the tumulus is estimate to date from the second half of the 4th century. {{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The '''Takeneyama Kofun''' (高根山古墳) is a {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) with a diameter of 52 meters and height of eight meters. It is also located to the south of the railroad tracks, and is the third largest in the prefecture. The surface was covered with ''fukiishi'' and both cylindrical and pot-shaped ''hanawa''. It was built at the end of the 4th century. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from 1997 to 1998. The '''Mikuridōyama Kofun''' (御厨堂山古墳) is a [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]] with total length of 34.5 meters and height of four meters. A bronze mirror and an iron sword have been recovered from its interior. It is also located to the south of the railroad tracks. It was excavated from 1997 to 1998. The '''Inariyama Kofun''' (稲荷山古墳) and the '''Akihayama Kofun''' (秋葉山古墳) are both located to the north of the [[Tōkaidō Main Line]] railway tracks, behind the Buddhist temple of Renjō-ji, and are survivors of what was once a group of four tumuli. The Inariyama Kofun is a 48-meter long, six meter high keyhole-shaped tumulus and the Akihayama Kofun is a 54-meter diameter, eight meter high circular tumulus. The now-vanished '''Kyōzuka Kofun''' (経塚古墳) was a keyhole-shaped tomb with a length of 90 meters, but was destroyed in the early [[Meiji period]] to make way for the Tōkaidō Main Line railway, during which time an [[Shinju-kyo|ancient bronze mirror]] was recovered. Jar-shaped ''haniwa'' have been recovered from the vicinity. The site is located a five-minute walk from the "Shinmei-mae" bus stop on the Entetsu Bus from [[Iwata Station (Shizuoka)|Iwata Station]] on the [[JR East]] Tōkaidō Main Line. ==Gallery== File:Takaneyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Takaneyama Kofun File:松林山古墳出土 三角縁二神二獣鏡 (模造).JPG|bronze mirror from the Shorinzan Kofun {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+Mikuriyama Kofun group !name !location !Coordinates !type !Size !Artifacts !Date !Notes |- |colspan=8 style="background-color:#C0C0C0;text-align:left"|'''Covered by National Historic Site designation''' |- |'''Shorinzan Kofun'''||rowspan=3|Shingai||{{coords|34|43|10.48|N|137|53|16.70|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark}}||keyhole||length = 107m||bronze mirrors, weapons, beads, ''haniwa''||late 4th century||style="text-align:left"|Excavated 1931 |- |'''Inariyama Kofun'''||{{coords|34|43|22.55|N|137|53|23.23|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark}}||keyhole||length = 48m||pottery||late 4th century||style="text-align:left"|Excavated 1997-1998 |- |'''Akihayama Kofun'''||{{coords|34|43|20.80|N|137|53|23.20|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark}}||Circular||dia = 54m||pottery||late 4th century||style="text-align:left"|Excavated 1997-1998 |- |'''Takaneyama Kofun'''||rowspan=2|Kamata||{{coords|34|43|8.30|N|137|53|20.80|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|}}||Circular||dia = 52m||''haniwa''||4th century||style="text-align:left"|Excavated 1997-1998 |- |'''Mikuridōyama Kofun'''||{{coords|34|43|1.05|N|137|53|27.85|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|}}||keyhole||length = 50m||weapons||5th century||style="text-align:left"|early 20th century |- |colspan=8 style="background-color:#C0C0C0;text-align:left"|'''Others''' |- |'''Kyozuka Kofun'''||rowspan=2|Shingai||{{coords|34|43|18.30|N|137|53|25.30|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark}}||keyhole||length = 90m||bronze mirror||early 4th century||style="text-align:left"|excavated 1985-1886; destroyed by railway construction |- |'''Shingai No.17 Kofun'''
（Renjoji No.5 Kofun)||{{coords|34|43|24.12|N|137|53|20.93|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|}}||circular||diameter = 30m||bronze mirror||early 5th century||style="text-align:left"|now destroyed |- |'''Mekakuyama Kofun'''||Kamata||{{coords|34|42|55.52|N|137|53|26.38|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|}}||Circular||35 x 33m|| ||5th century|| |} ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:Mikuriya Kofun Group}} *[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kunishitei/1002049.html Iwata city home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://kanko-iwata.jp/tanoshimu/tanoshimu-738/ Iwata City tourist information] {{in lang|ja}} ] {{Kofun navbar}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Mikuriyama Kofun group]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] }} [[en:Mikuriyama Kofun group]] [[sn:Mikuriyama Kofun group]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Mikuriyama Kofun group}}

{{Short description| part_of of kofun Kofuns in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Mikuriyama Kofun group | native_name = 御厨古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Shorinzan Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shorinzan Kofun | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mikuriyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|43|11|N|137|53|17|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Iwata, Shizuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōkai region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 3rd to 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Mikuriyama Kofun group'''|御厨古墳群|Mikuriyama Kofun gun}} is group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located inbetween the Shingai and Kamata neighborhoods of the city of [[Iwata, Shizuoka]] in the [[Tōkai region]] of [[Japan]]. From the artifacts, the tumulus is estimate to date from the second half of the 4th century. {{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The '''Takeneyama Kofun''' (高根山古墳) is a {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) with a diameter of 52 meters and height of eight meters. It is also located to the south of the railroad tracks, and is the third largest in the prefecture. The surface was covered with ''fukiishi'' and both cylindrical and pot-shaped ''hanawa''. It was built at the end of the 4th century. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from 1997 to 1998. The '''Mikuridōyama Kofun''' (御厨堂山古墳) is a [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]] with total length of 34.5 meters and height of four meters. A bronze mirror and an iron sword have been recovered from its interior. It is also located to the south of the railroad tracks. It was excavated from 1997 to 1998. The '''Inariyama Kofun''' (稲荷山古墳) and the '''Akihayama Kofun''' (秋葉山古墳) are both located to the north of the [[Tōkaidō Main Line]] railway tracks, behind the Buddhist temple of Renjō-ji, and are survivors of what was once a group of four tumuli. The Inariyama Kofun is a 48-meter long, six meter high keyhole-shaped tumulus and the Akihayama Kofun is a 54-meter diameter, eight meter high circular tumulus. The now-vanished '''Kyōzuka Kofun''' (経塚古墳) was a keyhole-shaped tomb with a length of 90 meters, but was destroyed in the early [[Meiji period]] to make way for the Tōkaidō Main Line railway, during which time an [[Shinju-kyo|ancient bronze mirror]] was recovered. Jar-shaped ''haniwa'' have been recovered from the vicinity. The site is located a five-minute walk from the "Shinmei-mae" bus stop on the Entetsu Bus from [[Iwata Station (Shizuoka)|Iwata Station]] on the [[JR East]] Tōkaidō Main Line. ==Gallery== File:Takaneyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Takaneyama Kofun File:松林山古墳出土 三角縁二神二獣鏡 (模造).JPG|bronze mirror from the Shorinzan Kofun {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+Mikuriyama Kofun group !name !location !Coordinates !type !Size !Artifacts !Date !Notes |- |colspan=8 style="background-color:#C0C0C0;text-align:left"|'''Covered by National Historic Site designation''' |- |'''Shorinzan Kofun'''||rowspan=3|Shingai||{{coords|34|43|10.48|N|137|53|16.70|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark}}||keyhole||length = 107m||bronze mirrors, weapons, beads, ''haniwa''||late 4th century||style="text-align:left"|Excavated 1931 |- |'''Inariyama Kofun'''||{{coords|34|43|22.55|N|137|53|23.23|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark}}||keyhole||length = 48m||pottery||late 4th century||style="text-align:left"|Excavated 1997-1998 |- |'''Akihayama Kofun'''||{{coords|34|43|20.80|N|137|53|23.20|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark}}||Circular||dia = 54m||pottery||late 4th century||style="text-align:left"|Excavated 1997-1998 |- |'''Takaneyama Kofun'''||rowspan=2|Kamata||{{coords|34|43|8.30|N|137|53|20.80|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|}}||Circular||dia = 52m||''haniwa''||4th century||style="text-align:left"|Excavated 1997-1998 |- |'''Mikuridōyama Kofun'''||{{coords|34|43|1.05|N|137|53|27.85|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|}}||keyhole||length = 50m||weapons||5th century||style="text-align:left"|early 20th century |- |colspan=8 style="background-color:#C0C0C0;text-align:left"|'''Others''' |- |'''Kyozuka Kofun'''||rowspan=2|Shingai||{{coords|34|43|18.30|N|137|53|25.30|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark}}||keyhole||length = 90m||bronze mirror||early 4th century||style="text-align:left"|excavated 1985-1886; destroyed by railway construction |- |'''Shingai No.17 Kofun'''

{{Short description| part_of of kofun Kofuns in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Mikuriyama Kofun group | native_name = 御厨古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Shorinzan Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shorinzan Kofun | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mikuriyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|43|11|N|137|53|17|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Iwata, Shizuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōkai region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 3rd to 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Mikuriyama Kofun group'''|御厨古墳群|Mikuriyama Kofun gun}} is group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located inbetween the Shingai and Kamata neighborhoods of the city of [[Iwata, Shizuoka]] in the [[Tōkai region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2001.{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/192532|title=御厨古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} ==Overview== The site consists of five large Kofuns, located in the southeastern part of the Iwatahara Plateau on the eastern bank of the [[Tenryū River]], which are part of a large cluster of more than 500 ''kofun'', most of which have now been destroyed by urban encroachment and the construction of the [[Tōkaidō Main Line]] railway tracks, which cuts through the site. The '''Shorinzan Kofun''' (松林山古墳), with a total length of 107 meters, is the largest of the group. It is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located south of the railroad tracks and on the north side of Shimmei Junior High School. It has a 66.5 meter diameter posterior circular portion with a height of ten meters. An excavation was conducted in 1931, at which time a long stone [[burial chamber]] was discovered with a large number of [[grave goods]], including one triangular-rimmed [[bronze mirror]], one bronze mirror depicting four animals, two bronze mirrors depicting flowers, ''[[magatama]]'', tubular beads, weapons (straight swords, iron and bronze spears, daggers) and agricultural tools (axes, sickles, saws),and [[Sue pottery]]] pottery were discovered. The mound was covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and the remains of many ''[[haniwa]]'' were found. From the artifacts, the tumulus is estimate to date from the second half of the 4th century. {{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The '''Takeneyama Kofun''' (高根山古墳) is a {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) with a diameter of 52 meters and height of eight meters. It is also located to the south of the railroad tracks, and is the third largest in the prefecture. The surface was covered with ''fukiishi'' and both cylindrical and pot-shaped ''hanawa''. It was built at the end of the 4th century. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from 1997 to 1998. The '''Mikuridōyama Kofun''' (御厨堂山古墳) is a [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]] with total length of 34.5 meters and height of four meters. A bronze mirror and an iron sword have been recovered from its interior. It is also located to the south of the railroad tracks. It was excavated from 1997 to 1998. The '''Inariyama Kofun''' (稲荷山古墳) and the '''Akihayama Kofun''' (秋葉山古墳) are both located to the north of the [[Tōkaidō Main Line]] railway tracks, behind the Buddhist temple of Renjō-ji, and are survivors of what was once a group of four tumuli. The Inariyama Kofun is a 48-meter long, six meter high keyhole-shaped tumulus and the Akihayama Kofun is a 54-meter diameter, eight meter high circular tumulus. The now-vanished '''Kyōzuka Kofun''' (経塚古墳) was a keyhole-shaped tomb with a length of 90 meters, but was destroyed in the early [[Meiji period]] to make way for the Tōkaidō Main Line railway, during which time an [[Shinju-kyo|ancient bronze mirror]] was recovered. Jar-shaped ''haniwa'' have been recovered from the vicinity. The site is located a five-minute walk from the "Shinmei-mae" bus stop on the Entetsu Bus from [[Iwata Station (Shizuoka)|Iwata Station]] on the [[JR East]] Tōkaidō Main Line. ==Gallery== File:Takaneyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Takaneyama Kofun File:松林山古墳出土 三角縁二神二獣鏡 (模造).JPG|bronze mirror from the Shorinzan Kofun {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+Mikuriyama Kofun group !name !location !Coordinates !type !Size !Artifacts !Date !Notes |- |colspan=8 style="background-color:#C0C0C0;text-align:left"|'''Covered by National Historic Site designation''' |- |'''Shorinzan Kofun'''||rowspan=3|Shingai||{{coords|34|43|10.48|N|137|53|16.70|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark}}||keyhole||length = 107m||bronze mirrors, weapons, beads, ''haniwa''||late 4th century||style="text-align:left"|Excavated 1931 |- |'''Inariyama Kofun'''||{{coords|34|43|22.55|N|137|53|23.23|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark}}||keyhole||length = 48m||pottery||late 4th century||style="text-align:left"|Excavated 1997-1998 |- |'''Akihayama Kofun'''||{{coords|34|43|20.80|N|137|53|23.20|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark}}||Circular||dia = 54m||pottery||late 4th century||style="text-align:left"|Excavated 1997-1998 |- |'''Takaneyama Kofun'''||rowspan=2|Kamata||{{coords|34|43|8.30|N|137|53|20.80|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|}}||Circular||dia = 52m||''haniwa''||4th century||style="text-align:left"|Excavated 1997-1998 |- |'''Mikuridōyama Kofun'''||{{coords|34|43|1.05|N|137|53|27.85|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|}}||keyhole||length = 50m||weapons||5th century||style="text-align:left"|early 20th century |- |colspan=8 style="background-color:#C0C0C0;text-align:left"|'''Others''' |- |'''Kyozuka Kofun'''||rowspan=2|Shingai||{{coords|34|43|18.30|N|137|53|25.30|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark}}||keyhole||length = 90m||bronze mirror||early 4th century||style="text-align:left"|excavated 1985-1886; destroyed by railway construction |- |'''Shingai No.17 Kofun'''
（Renjoji No.5 Kofun)||{{coords|34|43|24.12|N|137|53|20.93|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|}}||circular||diameter = 30m||bronze mirror||early 5th century||style="text-align:left"|now destroyed |- |'''Mekakuyama Kofun'''||Kamata||{{coords|34|42|55.52|N|137|53|26.38|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|}}||Circular||35 x 33m|| ||5th century|| |} ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:Mikuriya Kofun Group}} *[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kunishitei/1002049.html Iwata city home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://kanko-iwata.jp/tanoshimu/tanoshimu-738/ Iwata City tourist information] {{in lang|ja}} ] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mikuriyama Kofun group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mikuriyama Kofun group]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Mikuriyama Kofun group]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]]{{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:qq]] }} [[en:Mikuriyama Kofun group]] [[sn:Mikuriyama Kofun group]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Mikuriyama Kofun group}}

Jar-shaped ''haniwa'' have been recovered from the vicinity. The site is located a five-minute walk from the "Shinmei-mae" bus stop on the Entetsu Bus from [[Iwata Station (Shizuoka)|Iwata Station]] on the [[JR East]] Tōkaidō Main Line. ==Gallery== File:Takaneyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Takaneyama Kofun File:松林山古墳出土 三角縁二神二獣鏡 (模造).JPG|bronze mirror from the Shorinzan Kofun {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+Mikuriyama Kofun group !name !location !Coordinates !type !Size !Artifacts !Date !Notes |- |colspan=8 style="background-color:#C0C0C0;text-align:left"|'''Covered by National Historic Site designation''' |- |'''Shorinzan Kofun'''||rowspan=3|Shingai||{{coords|34|43|10.48|N|137|53|16.70|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark}}||keyhole||length = 107m||bronze mirrors, weapons, beads, ''haniwa''||late 4th century||style="text-align:left"|Excavated 1931 |- |'''Inariyama Kofun'''||{{coords|34|43|22.55|N|137|53|23.23|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark}}||keyhole||length = 48m||pottery||late 4th century||style="text-align:left"|Excavated 1997-1998 |- |'''Akihayama Kofun'''||{{coords|34|43|20.80|N|137|53|23.20|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark}}||Circular||dia = 54m||pottery||late 4th century||style="text-align:left"|Excavated 1997-1998 |- |'''Takaneyama Kofun'''||rowspan=2|Kamata||{{coords|34|43|8.30|N|137|53|20.80|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|}}||Circular||dia = 52m||''haniwa''||4th century||style="text-align:left"|Excavated 1997-1998 |- |'''Mikuridōyama Kofun'''||{{coords|34|43|1.05|N|137|53|27.85|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|}}||keyhole||length = 50m||weapons||5th century||style="text-align:left"|early 20th century |- |colspan=8 style="background-color:#C0C0C0;text-align:left"|'''Others''' |- |'''Kyozuka Kofun'''||rowspan=2|Shingai||{{coords|34|43|18.30|N|137|53|25.30|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark}}||keyhole||length = 90m||bronze mirror||early 4th century||style="text-align:left"|excavated 1985-1886; destroyed by railway construction |- |'''Shingai No.17 Kofun'''
（Renjoji No.5 Kofun)||{{coords|34|43|24.12|N|137|53|20.93|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|}}||circular||diameter = 30m||bronze mirror||early 5th century||style="text-align:left"|now destroyed |- |'''Mekakuyama Kofun'''||Kamata||{{coords|34|42|55.52|N|137|53|26.38|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|}}||Circular||35 x 33m|| ||5th century|| |} ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:Mikuriya Kofun Group}} *[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kunishitei/1002049.html Iwata city home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://kanko-iwata.jp/tanoshimu/tanoshimu-738/ Iwata City tourist information] {{in lang|ja}} ] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mikuriyama Kofun group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mikuriyama Kofun group]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Mikuriyama Kofun group]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[en:Mikuriyama Kofun group]] [[sn:Mikuriyama Kofun group]] {{citation needed|Mikuriyama Kofun group}} {{Kofun navbar}}

{{Short description| part_of of kofun Kofuns in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Mikuriyama Kofun group | native_name = 御厨古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Shorinzan Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shorinzan Kofun | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mikuriyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|43|11|N|137|53|17|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Iwata, Shizuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōkai region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 3rd to 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Mikuriyama Kofun group'''|御厨古墳群|Mikuriyama Kofun gun}} is group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located inbetween the Shingai and Kamata neighborhoods of the city of [[Iwata, Shizuoka]] in the [[Tōkai region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2001.{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/192532|title=御厨古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} ==Overview== The site consists of five large Kofuns, located in the southeastern part of the Iwatahara Plateau on the eastern bank of the [[Tenryū River]], which are part of a large cluster of more than 500 ''kofun'', most of which have now been destroyed by urban encroachment and the construction of the [[Tōkaidō Main Line]] railway tracks, which cuts through the site. The '''Shorinzan Kofun''' (松林山古墳), with a total length of 107 meters, is the largest of the group. It is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located south of the railroad tracks and on the north side of Shimmei Junior High School. It has a 66.5 meter diameter posterior circular portion with a height of ten meters. An excavation was conducted in 1931, at which time a long stone [[burial chamber]] was discovered with a large number of [[grave goods]], including one triangular-rimmed [[bronze mirror]], one bronze mirror depicting four animals, two bronze mirrors depicting flowers, ''[[magatama]]'', tubular beads, weapons (straight swords, iron and bronze spears, daggers) and agricultural tools (axes, sickles, saws),and [[Sue pottery]]] pottery were discovered. The mound was covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and the remains of many ''[[haniwa]]'' were found. From the artifacts, the tumulus is estimate to date from the second half of the 4th century. {{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The '''Takeneyama Kofun''' (高根山古墳) is a {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) with a diameter of 52 meters and height of eight meters. It is also located to the south of the railroad tracks, and is the third largest in the prefecture. The surface was covered with ''fukiishi'' and both cylindrical and pot-shaped ''hanawa''. It was built at the end of the 4th century. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from 1997 to 1998. The '''Mikuridōyama Kofun''' (御厨堂山古墳) is a [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]] with total length of 34.5 meters and height of four meters. A bronze mirror and an iron sword have been recovered from its interior. It is also located to the south of the railroad tracks. It was excavated from 1997 to 1998. The '''Inariyama Kofun''' (稲荷山古墳) and the '''Akihayama Kofun''' (秋葉山古墳) are both located to the north of the [[Tōkaidō Main Line]] railway tracks, behind the Buddhist temple of Renjō-ji, and are survivors of what was once a group of four tumuli. The Inariyama Kofun is a 48-meter long, six meter high keyhole-shaped tumulus and the Akihayama Kofun is a 54-meter diameter, eight meter high circular tumulus. The now-vanished '''Kyōzuka Kofun''' (経塚古墳) was a keyhole-shaped tomb with a length of 90 meters, but was destroyed in the early [[Meiji period]] to make way for the Tōkaidō Main Line railway, during which time an [[Shinju-kyo|ancient bronze mirror]] was recovered. Jar-shaped ''haniwa'' have been recovered from the vicinity. The site is located a five-minute walk from the "Shinmei-mae" bus stop on the Entetsu Bus from [[Iwata Station (Shizuoka)|Iwata Station]] on the [[JR East]] Tōkaidō Main Line. ==Gallery== File:Takaneyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Takaneyama Kofun File:松林山古墳出土 三角縁二神二獣鏡 (模造).JPG|bronze mirror from the Shorinzan Kofun {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+Mikuriyama Kofun group !name !location !Coordinates !type !Size !Artifacts !Date !Notes |- |colspan=8 style="background-color:#C0C0C0;text-align:left"|'''Covered by National Historic Site designation''' |- |'''Shorinzan Kofun'''||rowspan=3|Shingai||{{coords|34|43|10.48|N|137|53|16.70|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark}}||keyhole||length = 107m||bronze mirrors, weapons, beads, ''haniwa''||late 4th century||style="text-align:left"|Excavated 1931 |- |'''Inariyama Kofun'''||{{coords|34|43|22.55|N|137|53|23.23|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark}}||keyhole||length = 48m||pottery||late 4th century||style="text-align:left"|Excavated 1997-1998 |- |'''Akihayama Kofun'''||{{coords|34|43|20.80|N|137|53|23.20|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark}}||Circular||dia = 54m||pottery||late 4th century||style="text-align:left"|Excavated 1997-1998 |- |'''Takaneyama Kofun'''||rowspan=2|Kamata||{{coords|34|43|8.30|N|137|53|20.80|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|}}||Circular||dia = 52m||''haniwa''||4th century||style="text-align:left"|Excavated 1997-1998 |- |'''Mikuridōyama Kofun'''||{{coords|34|43|1.05|N|137|53|27.85|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|}}||keyhole||length = 50m||weapons||5th century||style="text-align:left"|early 20th century |- |colspan=8 style="background-color:#C0C0C0;text-align:left"|'''Others''' |- |'''Kyozuka Kofun'''||rowspan=2|Shingai||{{coords|34|43|18.30|N|137|53|25.30|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark}}||keyhole||length = 90m||bronze mirror||early 4th century||style="text-align:left"|excavated 1985-1886; destroyed by railway construction |- |'''Shingai No.17 Kofun'''
（Renjoji No.5 Kofun)||{{coords|34|43|24.12|N|137|53|20.93|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|}}||circular||diameter = 30m||bronze mirror||early 5th century||style="text-align:left"|now destroyed |- |'''Mekakuyama Kofun'''||Kamata||{{coords|34|42|55.52|N|137|53|26.38|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|}}||Circular||35 x 33m|| ||5th century|| |} ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:Mikuriya Kofun Group}} *[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kunishitei/1002049.html Iwata city home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://kanko-iwata.jp/tanoshimu/tanoshimu-738/ Iwata City tourist information] {{in lang|ja}} ] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mikuriyama Kofun group]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mikuriyama Kofun group]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Mikuriyama Kofun group]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[en:Mikuriyama Kofun group]] [[sn:Mikuriyama Kofun group]] {{citation needed|Mikuriyama Kofun group}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]]

{{Short description| part_of of kofun Kofuns in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Mikuriyama Kofun group | native_name = 御厨古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Shorinzan Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shorinzan Kofun | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mikuriyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|43|11|N|137|53|17|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Iwata, Shizuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōkai region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 3rd to 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Mikuriyama Kofun group'''|御厨古墳群|Mikuriyama Kofun gun}} is group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located inbetween the Shingai and Kamata neighborhoods of the city of [[Iwata, Shizuoka]] in the [[Tōkai region]] of [[Japan]]. The mound was covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and the remains of many ''[[haniwa]]'' were found. From the artifacts, the tumulus is estimate to date from the second half of the 4th century. {{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The '''Takeneyama Kofun''' (高根山古墳) is a {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) with a diameter of 52 meters and height of eight meters. It is also located to the south of the railroad tracks, and is the third largest in the prefecture. The surface was covered with ''fukiishi'' and both cylindrical and pot-shaped ''hanawa''. It was built at the end of the 4th century. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from 1997 to 1998. The '''Mikuridōyama Kofun''' (御厨堂山古墳) is a [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]] with total length of 34.5 meters and height of four meters. A bronze mirror and an iron sword have been recovered from its interior. It is also located to the south of the railroad tracks. It was excavated from 1997 to 1998. The '''Inariyama Kofun''' (稲荷山古墳) and the '''Akihayama Kofun''' (秋葉山古墳) are both located to the north of the [[Tōkaidō Main Line]] railway tracks, behind the Buddhist temple of Renjō-ji, and are survivors of what was once a group of four tumuli. The Inariyama Kofun is a 48-meter long, six meter high keyhole-shaped tumulus and the Akihayama Kofun is a 54-meter diameter, eight meter high circular tumulus. The now-vanished '''Kyōzuka Kofun''' (経塚古墳) was a keyhole-shaped tomb with a length of 90 meters, but was destroyed in the early [[Meiji period]] to make way for the Tōkaidō Main Line railway, during which time an [[Shinju-kyo|ancient bronze mirror]] was recovered. Jar-shaped ''haniwa'' have been recovered from the vicinity. The site is located a five-minute walk from the "Shinmei-mae" bus stop on the Entetsu Bus from [[Iwata Station (Shizuoka)|Iwata Station]] on the [[JR East]] Tōkaidō Main Line. ==Gallery== File:Takaneyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Takaneyama Kofun File:松林山古墳出土 三角縁二神二獣鏡 (模造).JPG|bronze mirror from the Shorinzan Kofun {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+Mikuriyama Kofun group !name !location !Coordinates !type !Size !Artifacts !Date !Notes |- |colspan=8 style="background-color:#C0C0C0;text-align:left"|'''Covered by National Historic Site designation''' |- |'''Shorinzan Kofun'''||rowspan=3|Shingai||{{coords|34|43|10.48|N|137|53|16.70|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark}}||keyhole||length = 107m||bronze mirrors, weapons, beads, ''haniwa''||late 4th century||style="text-align:left"|Excavated 1931 |- |'''Inariyama Kofun'''||{{coords|34|43|22.55|N|137|53|23.23|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark}}||keyhole||length = 48m||pottery||late 4th century||style="text-align:left"|Excavated 1997-1998 |- |'''Akihayama Kofun'''||{{coords|34|43|20.80|N|137|53|23.20|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark}}||Circular||dia = 54m||pottery||late 4th century||style="text-align:left"|Excavated 1997-1998 |- |'''Takaneyama Kofun'''||rowspan=2|Kamata||{{coords|34|43|8.30|N|137|53|20.80|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|}}||Circular||dia = 52m||''haniwa''||4th century||style="text-align:left"|Excavated 1997-1998 |- |'''Mikuridōyama Kofun'''||{{coords|34|43|1.05|N|137|53|27.85|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|}}||keyhole||length = 50m||weapons||5th century||style="text-align:left"|early 20th century |- |colspan=8 style="background-color:#C0C0C0;text-align:left"|'''Others''' |- |'''Kyozuka Kofun'''||rowspan=2|Shingai||{{coords|34|43|18.30|N|137|53|25.30|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark}}||keyhole||length = 90m||bronze mirror||early 4th century||style="text-align:left"|excavated 1985-1886; destroyed by railway construction |- |'''Shingai No.17 Kofun'''

{{Short description| part_of of kofun Kofuns in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Mikuriyama Kofun group | native_name = 御厨古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Shorinzan Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shorinzan Kofun | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mikuriyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|43|11|N|137|53|17|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Iwata, Shizuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōkai region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 3rd to 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Mikuriyama Kofun group'''|御厨古墳群|Mikuriyama Kofun gun}} is group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located inbetween the Shingai and Kamata neighborhoods of the city of [[Iwata, Shizuoka]] in the [[Tōkai region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2001.{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/192532|title=御厨古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} ==Overview== The site consists of five large Kofuns, located in the southeastern part of the Iwatahara Plateau on the eastern bank of the [[Tenryū River]], which are part of a large cluster of more than 500 ''kofun'', most of which have now been destroyed by urban encroachment and the construction of the [[Tōkaidō Main Line]] railway tracks, which cuts through the site. The '''Shorinzan Kofun''' (松林山古墳), with a total length of 107 meters, is the largest of the group. It is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The mound was covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and the remains of many ''[[haniwa]]'' were found. From the artifacts, the tumulus is estimate to date from the second half of the 4th century. {{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The '''Takeneyama Kofun''' (高根山古墳) is a {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) with a diameter of 52 meters and height of eight meters. It is also located to the south of the railroad tracks, and is the third largest in the prefecture. The surface was covered with ''fukiishi'' and both cylindrical and pot-shaped ''hanawa''. It was built at the end of the 4th century. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from 1997 to 1998. Jar-shaped ''haniwa'' have been recovered from the vicinity. The site is located a five-minute walk from the "Shinmei-mae" bus stop on the Entetsu Bus from [[Iwata Station (Shizuoka)|Iwata Station]] on the [[JR East]] Tōkaidō Main Line. ==Gallery== File:Takaneyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Takaneyama Kofun File:松林山古墳出土 三角縁二神二獣鏡 (模造).JPG|bronze mirror from the Shorinzan Kofun {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+Mikuriyama Kofun group !name !location !Coordinates !type !Size !Artifacts !Date !Notes |- |colspan=8 style="background-color:#C0C0C0;text-align:left"|'''Covered by National Historic Site designation''' |- |'''Shorinzan Kofun'''||rowspan=3|Shingai||{{coords|34|43|10.48|N|137|53|16.70|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark}}||keyhole||length = 107m||bronze mirrors, weapons, beads, ''haniwa''||late 4th century||style="text-align:left"|Excavated 1931 |- |'''Inariyama Kofun'''||{{coords|34|43|22.55|N|137|53|23.23|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark}}||keyhole||length = 48m||pottery||late 4th century||style="text-align:left"|Excavated 1997-1998 |- |'''Akihayama Kofun'''||{{coords|34|43|20.80|N|137|53|23.20|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark}}||Circular||dia = 54m||pottery||late 4th century||style="text-align:left"|Excavated 1997-1998 |- |'''Takaneyama Kofun'''||rowspan=2|Kamata||{{coords|34|43|8.30|N|137|53|20.80|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|}}||Circular||dia = 52m||''haniwa''||4th century||style="text-align:left"|Excavated 1997-1998 |- |'''Mikuridōyama Kofun'''||{{coords|34|43|1.05|N|137|53|27.85|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|}}||keyhole||length = 50m||weapons||5th century||style="text-align:left"|early 20th century |- |colspan=8 style="background-color:#C0C0C0;text-align:left"|'''Others''' |- |'''Kyozuka Kofun'''||rowspan=2|Shingai||{{coords|34|43|18.30|N|137|53|25.30|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark}}||keyhole||length = 90m||bronze mirror||early 4th century||style="text-align:left"|excavated 1985-1886; destroyed by railway construction |- |'''Shingai No.17 Kofun'''

{{Short description| part_of of kofun Kofuns in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Mikuriyama Kofun group | native_name = 御厨古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Shorinzan Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shorinzan Kofun | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mikuriyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|43|11|N|137|53|17|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Iwata, Shizuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōkai region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 3rd to 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Mikuriyama Kofun group'''|御厨古墳群|Mikuriyama Kofun gun}} is group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located inbetween the Shingai and Kamata neighborhoods of the city of [[Iwata, Shizuoka]] in the [[Tōkai region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2001.{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/192532|title=御厨古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} ==Overview== The site consists of five large Kofuns, located in the southeastern part of the Iwatahara Plateau on the eastern bank of the [[Tenryū River]], which are part of a large cluster of more than 500 ''kofun'', most of which have now been destroyed by urban encroachment and the construction of the [[Tōkaidō Main Line]] railway tracks, which cuts through the site. The '''Shorinzan Kofun''' (松林山古墳), with a total length of 107 meters, is the largest of the group. It is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located south of the railroad tracks and on the north side of Shimmei Junior High School. It has a 66.5 meter diameter posterior circular portion with a height of ten meters. An excavation was conducted in 1931, at which time a long stone [[burial chamber]] was discovered with a large number of [[grave goods]], including one triangular-rimmed [[bronze mirror]], one bronze mirror depicting four animals, two bronze mirrors depicting flowers, ''[[magatama]]'', tubular beads, weapons (straight swords, iron and bronze spears, daggers) and agricultural tools (axes, sickles, saws),and [[Sue pottery]]] pottery were discovered. The mound was covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and the remains of many ''[[haniwa]]'' were found. From the artifacts, the tumulus is estimate to date from the second half of the 4th century. {{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The '''Takeneyama Kofun''' (高根山古墳) is a {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) with a diameter of 52 meters and height of eight meters. It is also located to the south of the railroad tracks, and is the third largest in the prefecture. The surface was covered with ''fukiishi'' and both cylindrical and pot-shaped ''hanawa''. It was built at the end of the 4th century. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from 1997 to 1998. The '''Mikuridōyama Kofun''' (御厨堂山古墳) is a [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]] with total length of 34.5 meters and height of four meters. A bronze mirror and an iron sword have been recovered from its interior. It is also located to the south of the railroad tracks. It was excavated from 1997 to 1998. The '''Inariyama Kofun''' (稲荷山古墳) and the '''Akihayama Kofun''' (秋葉山古墳) are both located to the north of the [[Tōkaidō Main Line]] railway tracks, behind the Buddhist temple of Renjō-ji, and are survivors of what was once a group of four tumuli. The Inariyama Kofun is a 48-meter long, six meter high keyhole-shaped tumulus and the Akihayama Kofun is a 54-meter diameter, eight meter high circular tumulus. The now-vanished '''Kyōzuka Kofun''' (経塚古墳) was a keyhole-shaped tomb with a length of 90 meters, but was destroyed in the early [[Meiji period]] to make way for the Tōkaidō Main Line railway, during which time an [[Shinju-kyo|ancient bronze mirror]] was recovered. Jar-shaped ''haniwa'' have been recovered from the vicinity. The site is located a five-minute walk from the "Shinmei-mae" bus stop on the Entetsu Bus from [[Iwata Station (Shizuoka)|Iwata Station]] on the [[JR East]] Tōkaidō Main Line. ==Gallery== File:Takaneyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Takaneyama Kofun File:松林山古墳出土 三角縁二神二獣鏡 (模造).JPG|bronze mirror from the Shorinzan Kofun {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+Mikuriyama Kofun group !name !location !Coordinates !type !Size !Artifacts !Date !Notes |- |colspan=8 style="background-color:#C0C0C0;text-align:left"|'''Covered by National Historic Site designation''' |- |'''Shorinzan Kofun'''||rowspan=3|Shingai||{{coords|34|43|10.48|N|137|53|16.70|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark}}||keyhole||length = 107m||bronze mirrors, weapons, beads, ''haniwa''||late 4th century||style="text-align:left"|Excavated 1931 |- |'''Inariyama Kofun'''||{{coords|34|43|22.55|N|137|53|23.23|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark}}||keyhole||length = 48m||pottery||late 4th century||style="text-align:left"|Excavated 1997-1998 |- |'''Akihayama Kofun'''||{{coords|34|43|20.80|N|137|53|23.20|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark}}||Circular||dia = 54m||pottery||late 4th century||style="text-align:left"|Excavated 1997-1998 |- |'''Takaneyama Kofun'''||rowspan=2|Kamata||{{coords|34|43|8.30|N|137|53|20.80|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|}}||Circular||dia = 52m||''haniwa''||4th century||style="text-align:left"|Excavated 1997-1998 |- |'''Mikuridōyama Kofun'''||{{coords|34|43|1.05|N|137|53|27.85|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|}}||keyhole||length = 50m||weapons||5th century||style="text-align:left"|early 20th century |- |colspan=8 style="background-color:#C0C0C0;text-align:left"|'''Others''' |- |'''Kyozuka Kofun'''||rowspan=2|Shingai||{{coords|34|43|18.30|N|137|53|25.30|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark}}||keyhole||length = 90m||bronze mirror||early 4th century||style="text-align:left"|excavated 1985-1886; destroyed by railway construction |- |'''Shingai No.17 Kofun'''
（Renjoji No.5 Kofun)||{{coords|34|43|24.12|N|137|53|20.93|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|}}||circular||diameter = 30m||bronze mirror||early 5th century||style="text-align:left"|now destroyed |- |'''Mekakuyama Kofun'''||Kamata||{{coords|34|42|55.52|N|137|53|26.38|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|}}||Circular||35 x 33m|| ||5th century|| |} ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:Mikuriya Kofun Group}} *[https://www.city.iwata.shizuoka.jp/sports_midokoro/bunkazai/kunishitei/1002049.html Iwata city home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://kanko-iwata.jp/tanoshimu/tanoshimu-738/ Iwata City tourist information] {{in lang|ja}} ] {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Mikuriyama Kofun group}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:History of Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Mikuriyama Kofun group]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Mikuriyama Kofun group]] [[en:Mikuriyama Kofun group]]

Jar-shaped ''haniwa'' have been recovered from the vicinity.
